reasoning: Chrome's massive ~65% market share lead over its closest competitor (Safari at ~18%) makes an organic shift before the end of 2025 practically impossible. The only plausible threat to its dominance was structural intervention by the US government. However, Judge Mehta's September 2025 antitrust ruling explicitly rejected the DOJ's request to spin off Chrome, putting any potential legal breakups on hold for appeals that will take until at least 2027. Consequently, with no regulatory threats remaining for Q4 2025, and StatCounter (the Wikipedia table's source) historically showing extreme consistency over short timeframes, Chrome will undoubtedly remain the most popular browser at the year's end. The 2% reservation reflects negligible edge cases such as major Wikipedia editorial changes that remove or alter the resolution table, leading to an ambiguous or N/A resolution.
